html:
Wendy getting struck by lightning.
“SYSTEM OVERLOAD”

–WX-78

Lightning occurs naturally during heavy Rain, striking up to 10 units away from the player. Mobs and objects within a 3 unit radius of the point of touch-down can be set on Fire. Lightning can also strike the player through unnatural occurrences (none of which will cause any Health damage), such as Touch Stone resurrection, using a Telelocator Staff, or damaging an Ancient Pseudoscience Station.

A Lightning Rod can be used to safely attract lightning strikes within a 40 unit radius (about 10 Pitchfork tiles), and will emit Light for roughly two days after being struck.

Lightning can also initiate WX-78's System Overload perk, giving them a range of abilities, unless WX-78 equips an Umbrella or a Pretty Parasol. The Pretty Parasol cannot be set on fire by Lightning.

Downloadable Content

In all DLCs, lightning can strike the player, dealing 10-20 points of damage, depending on the player's Wetness. Wearing any piece of insulated clothing will prevent damage from lightning or electricity.

In Reign of Giants, Volt Goats will attract lightning strikes in the same manner as Lightning Rods. When struck, they become charged (making them aggressive). Charged Volt Goats deal additional electricity damage, and can drop Electric Milk when killed. Eyebrella, Rain Coat, and Rain Hat are the insulated items available in Reign of Giants.

In Shipwrecked, the Hurricane Season has very frequent lightning strikes during storms. While it isn't considered a lightning strike, when attacked, Jellyfish will also deal electricity damage to the attacker. Dumbrella, Snakeskin Jacket, and Snakeskin Hat are the insulated items available in Shipwrecked.

In Hamlet, lightning strikes occur during the Humid Season. Thunderbirds will attack the player and other creatures with three lightning strikes if they get too close to their nest.

Gallery

  • Wilson getting struck by lightning.
  • Willow getting struck by lightning.
  • Wolfgang (in all of his forms) getting struck by lightning.
  • Wendy getting struck by lightning.
  • WX-78 getting struck by lightning.
  • Wickerbottom getting struck by lightning.
  • Woodie getting struck by lightning.
  • Wes getting struck by lightning.
  • Maxwell getting struck by lightning.
  • Wigfrid getting struck by lightning.
  • Webber getting struck by lightning.
  • Walani getting struck by lightning.
  • Warly getting struck by lightning.
  • Wilbur getting struck by lightning.
  • Woodlegs getting struck by lightning.
  • Wheeler getting struck by lightning.
  • A single lightning bolt frame.
  • Wickerbottom summoning lightning by reading one of her Books, The End Is Nigh.
  • Lightning strike near the Ancient Pseudoscience Station.
  • A Volt Goat getting struck by lightning.

“SYSTEM OVERLOAD”
–WX-78
Lightning occurs naturally during heavy Rain, striking up to 10 units away from the player. Mobs and objects within a 3 unit radius of the point of touch-down can be set on Fire. Lightning can also strike the player through unnatural occurrences (none of which will cause any Health damage), such as Touch Stone resurrection, using a Telelocator Staff, or damaging an Ancient Pseudoscience Station.
A Lightning Rod can be used to safely attract lightning strikes within a 40 unit radius (about 10 Pitchfork tiles), and will emit Light for roughly two days after being struck.

Downloadable Content[edit | edit source]
“Safe from that frightening lightning!”
–Wilson, when lightning damage is avoided
“I'm safe from the sky-fire.”
–Willow, when lightning damage is avoided
“Lightning is weak compared to Wolfgang clothes!”
–Wolfgang, when lightning damage is avoided
“So close to death, yet so far.”
–Wendy, when lightning damage is avoided
“CIRCUIT INCOMPLETE. NO CHARGE RECEIVED.”
–WX-78, when lightning damage is avoided
“Thank goodness for this non-conductive clothing!”
–Wickerbottom, when lightning damage is avoided
“That tickled a bit, eh?”
–Woodie, when lightning damage is avoided
“Can't touch this!”
–Maxwell, when lightning damage is avoided
“I narrowly missed a spontaneous electrostatic discharge!”
–Wagstaff, when lightning damage is avoided
“I röde in ön a bölt öf lightning.”
–Wigfrid, when lightning damage is avoided
“We're invincible!”
–Webber, when lightning damage is avoided
“Not today, nature!”
–Walani, when lightning damage is avoided
“That was much too close!”
–Warly, when lightning damage is avoided
“Ye didn't get ol'Woodlegs, sky bolts!”
–Woodlegs, when lightning damage is avoided
“WILBA HAS'T ESCAPED SKY BOLTS!”
–Wilba, when lightning damage is avoided
“Too close!”
–Wormwood, when lightning damage is avoided
“Another daring escape!”
–Wheeler, when lightning damage is avoided
“Ha! Now you gotta kiss me!”
–Winona, when lightning damage is avoided
“Hyuyu! Can't catch me!”
–Wortox, when lightning damage is avoided
“GLORPT! Almost got sparky!”
–Wurt, when lightning damage is avoided
“Woah! I almost got zapped!”
–Walter, when lightning damage is avoided
“I was almost struck down!”
–Wanda, when lightning damage is avoided
In all DLCs, lightning can strike the player, dealing 10-20 points of damage, depending on the player's Wetness. Wearing any piece of insulated clothing will prevent damage from lightning or electricity.
In Reign of Giants, Volt Goats will attract lightning strikes in the same manner as Lightning Rods. When struck, they become charged (making them aggressive). Charged Volt Goats deal additional electricity damage, and can drop Electric Milk when killed. Eyebrella, Rain Coat, and Rain Hat are the insulated items available in Reign of Giants.
In Shipwrecked, the Hurricane Season has very frequent lightning strikes during storms. While it isn't considered a lightning strike, when attacked, Jellyfish will also deal electricity damage to the attacker. Dumbrella, Snakeskin Jacket, and Snakeskin Hat are the insulated items available in Shipwrecked.
In Hamlet, lightning strikes occur during the Humid Season. Thunderbirds will attack the player and other creatures with three lightning strikes if they get too close to their nest.

{{Quote|SYSTEM OVERLOAD|WX-78}}
'''Lightning''' occurs naturally during heavy [[Rain]], striking up to 10 units away from the player. [[Mobs]] and objects within a 3 unit radius of the point of touch-down can be set on [[Fire]]. Lightning can also strike the player through unnatural occurrences (none of which will cause any [[Health]] damage), such as [[Touch Stone]] resurrection, using a [[Telelocator Staff]], or damaging an [[Ancient Pseudoscience Station]].

A [[Lightning Rod]] can be used to safely attract lightning strikes within a 40 unit radius (about 10 [[Pitchfork]] tiles), and will [[Light sources|emit Light]] for roughly two days after being struck.

Lightning can also initiate [[WX-78]]'s [[WX-78#Special Power|System Overload]] perk, giving them a range of abilities, unless WX-78 equips an [[Umbrella]] or a [[Pretty Parasol]]. The Pretty Parasol cannot be set on fire by Lightning.
